words.russian: ходить по кругу, топтаться на месте
words.example_en: I've been going around in circles trying to write this report, but I haven't been given enough information.
After losing a few games earlier this month, the team is back on track now.
Ben suffered the consequences of his misbehavior and had to wash the car.
The party got out of hand, so the neighbors called the police.
The barbecue was an utter failure. It rained and none of my friends turned up.
The decorators have really screwed up this job! We won't use them again.
Kyle and Erin got lost in the forest when they couldn't read their map.
It was sunny today, but the weather is going to get worse tomorrow.
The rangers have put a stop to people driving through the park to cut pollution.
Joshua's dog has gone missing. He's putting up signs around town.

words.russian: быть на грани, чуть не сделать
words.example_en: The climbers came close to reaching the peak but were too tired to continue.
Don't stand so close to the stove. Your clothes could catch fire!
The boys were caught red-handed when they tried to break into the house.
The flood did a lot of damage to our house, so we had to move out.
Lucia has a vivid imagination. Her artworks are so original.
Gustav has a selfish streak. He won't share his cookies with his friends.
My dad's just an average Joe. He enjoys burgers and fries, and loves watching baseball.
Noah is really down to earth. He earns lots of money, but he lives in a little cottage.
I know Rex looks a bit scary, but he wouldn't hurt a fly.
Claire is a social butterfly. She's always going to parties.
My uncle's a lone wolf. He lives on his own in a cabin in the forest.
Jim's a good egg. He helped me to move last week.
Debbie's a tough cookie. She doesn't mind when people criticize her work.
Kirsty remained as cool as a cucumber throughout her performance.

words.russian: струсил, сдрейфил и не сделал что-то
words.example_en: I chickened out of diving off the top board in the pool. It was too high!

words.russian: диванный овощ — ленивый человек, много смотрящий телевизор
words.example_en: Jennifer is such a couch potato. She never goes out and watches TV all weekend instead.
My dog behaved beautifully when I took him on the train.
Henry can't control his badly behaved students.

words.russian: начать новую жизнь, с чистого листа
words.example_en: Tim's turned over a new leaf. He used to watch TV in the evenings, but now he goes jogging.
It might rain later. I'll play it safe and take an umbrella.

words.russian: семь раз отмерь, один раз отрежь
words.example_en: Are you sure you want to buy that motorcycle? I think you should look before you leap.
I always wear goggles when I do an experiment. Better safe than sorry!
I love looking after my niece. She's a ray of sunshine.

words.english: have your head in the clouds
words.example_en: You weren't listening to me - you had your head in the clouds.
He talks big, but never finishes anything he starts.
Be careful, my uncle will talk your ear off.
I respect him because he always speaks his mind.
The lecturer kept droning on about statistics for an hour.
Nathan came clean with his parents and told them he had broken the window.
My teacher was brutally honest with me and told me my essay was terrible.

words.russian: сказать невинную ложь, ложь во спасение
words.example_en: I thought Bella's hat was ridiculous, but I told her a white lie and said I loved it.
Our boss says the company is doing well, but she has a reputation for telling half-truths.
Bastian kept a close watch on his children as they played outside.
Karen has an eagle eye. She found lots of mistakes when I asked her to check my essay.
Juanita is so careful at work. She does everything by the book.

words.russian: заладил одно и то же, как заезженная пластинка
words.example_en: Dad's always telling me to clean my room. He is like a broken record.
Trent is a teacher's pet. He always offers to carry the teacher's bag.
Erik was so lazy. He didn't lift a finger to help us clean up after the party.
Danielle decided to bend the rules and let her kids stay up later than usual.
Paul has really crossed the line this time. He's eaten all his sister's birthday cake.
Pedro's father put his foot down and refused to let him watch TV.
The manager encouraged her team to think outside the box.
She's a real team player and always helps her colleagues.
The teacher asked the students to keep quiet during the test.
Paul is as quiet as a mouse. He never talks in meetings.
Naomi was as brave as a lion and confronted the school bully in the playground.
My grandfather is as wise as an owl. He always gives good advice.

words.russian: лёгкая рука в садоводстве, талант выращивать растения
words.example_en: Paula has a green thumb. She has so many beautiful plants in her house.

words.english: look like you've seen a ghost
words.example_en: Rita looked like she'd seen a ghost after I told her my news.
Country music is not my cup of tea. I prefer rock music.
I really feel for Mario. He has to work so hard in the kitchen.

words.russian: струсить, дать слабину перед важным делом
words.example_en: Caitlin got cold feet about singing karaoke.
Leo cried his eyes out when I said he couldn't have another cookie.
It was a weight off my shoulders when Mark found my cat.
Liz was feeling blue when her best friend left the country.
My neighbor saw red when I parked in front of his house.
I was green with envy when Jamila showed me her new house.
Silvio went as white as a sheet when he got the bad news.
The children all went bananas when I took them to the playground.
This terrible weather is driving me nuts.
Clare was on cloud nine after she was promoted.
Jack was on top of the world when his team won the trophy.
Dan grinned from ear to ear when he read his test results.
The news that our profits were up was music to our ears.
I was over the moon when I won the best actor award.
My motorcycle is my pride and joy. I clean it every day.
The kids jumped for joy when I brought our new puppy home.
Brendan wept with joy when he won the talent competition.

words.english: on the edge of your seat
words.russian: как на иголках, в напряжённом ожидании
words.example_en: I was on the edge of my seat as the detective revealed the murderer.
Sally was bursting with excitement as her favorite band came on stage.
Jamal had butterflies in his stomach as he waited for his date outside the restaurant.
Joseph has been down in the dumps since his girlfriend left him.

words.russian: довести до слёз, не сдержать слёз
words.example_en: Carlos was reduced to tears when Marta was mean about his cooking.
Jason's loud music is really driving me crazy.
Christine felt on edge as she waited to see her dentist.
Stella lost face when she got the figures wrong in her presentation.
I almost died of shame when my girlfriend found me singing in front of the mirror.
Maureen was a bundle of nerves before her wedding.
Keep your chin up! I'm sure the weather will get better soon.
I wish Nathan would turn his music down. It's getting on my nerves.
My heart sank when I got to the museum and saw that it was closed.
My girlfriend caught me off guard when she said two of her friends would be joining us for dinner tonight.
After months of boring lessons, our new teacher was a breath of fresh air.
The piano teacher had a hunch that Jordan hadn't done any practice since his last lesson.
I completely zoned out during that meeting. It was so boring!
It was music to my ears when my son told me he'd passed his driving test.
Kira's boss made a scene when she arrived five minutes late.
The sight of the cake in the café window made my mouth water.
Tiffany had fun at the festival with her friends.
Robin always gets angry when his children refuse to eat their food.
My kids go crazy whenever they hear that song.
Natalie always keeps calm when she has to talk to angry customers.
Fiona looked as proud as a peacock in her new hat.
I was shaking like a leaf before I went into the cave.
Ela turned beet-red when I caught her copying my homework.
I always get the blues when my vacation is over and I have to go back to work.
After climbing the hill, I realized I'm no spring chicken!
My assistant is a bit wet behind the ears. He still has a lot to learn.
These bright young things have just started college.

words.russian: стирать разрыв поколений, нравиться людям всех возрастов
words.example_en: This TV show bridges the generation gap. The whole family loves it.
I've just turned 40, and I'm in the prime of my life. I always go to the gym after work.
Katie started learning piano when she was knee-high to a grasshopper.
I'm too long in the tooth to try out skydiving.
Derek and Joan spent their twilight years traveling the world.
I can't come to that nightclub with you! I'm over the hill.
In many countries, there is a big celebration when young people come of age.

words.english: by the skin of your teeth
words.example_en: Maddy caught the train by the skin of her teeth. It left a few seconds later.
We'll have to take a rain check on meeting up tonight.

words.russian: раз в сто лет, очень редко
words.example_en: Huan only cooks at home once in a blue moon.
You'll have to wait until the cows come home before Yuri fixes your car.
Finley's growing up so quickly! He was walking before we knew it.
We were up at the crack of dawn to catch our flight.
Fred stayed up until the wee hours, chatting with his grandfather.

words.russian: в последний момент, в самый последний миг
words.example_en: We arrived in the nick of time. The train was about to leave.

words.english: at the drop of a hat
words.russian: в мгновение ока, по первому сигналу
words.example_en: My cousin is very emotional. He'll start an argument at the drop of a hat.
You really need to get going if you want to catch the train.

words.english: all good things come to those who wait
words.example_en: Keep saving patiently—all good things come to those who wait.

words.russian: время тянется медленно, когда с нетерпением чего-то ждёшь
words.example_en: Stop checking the oven every minute—a watched pot never boils.

words.english: there's no time like the present
words.example_en: Stop putting it off—there's no time like the present.
Rebecca has blue blood. Her great-grandmother was a princess.

words.english: be born with a silver spoon in your mouth
words.russian: родиться с серебряной ложкой во рту
words.example_en: Philip was born with a silver spoon in his mouth. He grew up in a castle in France.
Bob is from the wrong side of the tracks.

words.english: not have two pennies to rub together
words.russian: не иметь ни гроша за душой
words.example_en: My parents didn't have two pennies to rub together, but we still had lots of fun.
Sarah had to pay through the nose for a taxi home after she missed the last train.
Luna wants to buy a house soon, so she's building up a nest-egg.
Donna struck it rich when she won the lottery.
If you don't turn off the lights when you leave the house, you're throwing money down the drain.
It was really generous of Imran to buy us dinner at that expensive restaurant, but he does like to throw money around.
We ended up out of pocket when no one wanted to buy our ice cream.
Mike is rolling in it. He's just bought himself another yacht.
Tourists usually pay over the odds when they buy souvenirs.

words.english: cost an arm and a leg
words.russian: влететь в копеечку, стоить бешеных денег
words.example_en: My new suit cost an arm and a leg. I bought it from a tailor in Milan.
A valuable painting went under the hammer and sold for £3 million last week.
Javier drove a hard bargain, refusing to drop the price of his house.
The taxi from the airport cost more than $70. It was highway robbery!
This store sells designer labels at bargain-basement prices.
Cathy had been lining her pockets with company funds for years before the boss caught her.
The small business has been in the red for months.
Boris was given a golden handshake when he retired from the company.
Both parents work hard to bring home the bacon.
Freelance writing is my bread and butter these days.
That product line has become a real cash cow.
Ordinary workers resent the fat cats at the top.

words.russian: самому оплачивать свои расходы, не быть в тягость
words.example_en: I live with my parents, but pay my way by buying groceries.
The plumber gave us a ballpark figure for the cost of the new bathroom.
Leo bears a striking resemblance to his grandfather.
Karen is the spitting image of that actor in the new sci-fi film.

words.russian: мрут как мухи — массово заболевают или выбывают
words.example_en: My colleagues all have terrible colds. They're dropping like flies!
I advised Kathy not to go into the water. It would be risking life and limb.
Leo was feeling the worse for wear after he stayed out all night.
My hay fever tends to flare up every summer.
Ollie received a clean bill of health from his doctor.
The dancers in this show have to be as fit as a fiddle.
Moving to the country has given Kira a new lease on life.
A week at a health spa was just what the doctor ordered after a stressful month at work.
After a few days by the coast, Lauren began to feel as right as rain.
Imran looks the picture of health since he started going to the gym.
Oscar had to take a fitness test before working as a police officer.
My grandmother is as deaf as a post. She always turns up the TV really loud.
Selma slept like a log after a long day climbing in the Alps.
He fell off his bike but seemed no worse for wear.
I can't believe I forgot to bring the sandwiches! I've got a memory like a sieve.

words.english: go in one ear and out the other
words.russian: в одно ухо влетело, в другое вылетело
words.example_en: That boring lecture went in one ear and out the other!

words.russian: сбиться с мысли, потерять нить рассуждения
words.example_en: Jacob was explaining the diagram when he suddenly lost his train of thought.
It's the worst winter in living memory. I've never seen so much snow.
The police showed me a photo of the suspect to jog my memory.
I've seen that animal before, but its name escapes me.
I've been racking my brain all morning but can't remember where I put my passport.

words.english: at the back of your mind
words.example_en: I enjoyed the party, but at the back of my mind I knew I had to catch the last train home.
When Betty found her old teddy bear, memories of her childhood came flooding back.
Looking at my old medals stirred up memories of competing in the Olympics.

words.english: take a trip down memory lane
words.russian: окунуться в воспоминания, пройтись по волнам памяти
words.example_en: Emi and Sofia took a trip down memory lane as they talked about their school days.
As a rule of thumb, it takes about 10 minutes to cook pasta.
You've been to Bali, haven't you? Can I pick your brain about the best places to visit?

words.english: get to the bottom of something
words.example_en: The plumber got to the bottom of what's causing the leak. We need to replace the pipe.
Working on the farm meant I had to come to grips with driving a tractor.
Phil showed me around the kitchen. He knows the nuts and bolts of running a restaurant.

words.russian: вдалбливать в голову, твердить снова и снова
words.example_en: How many times must I drum it into your head?

words.russian: выше моего понимания, не понял ни слова
words.example_en: The professor's astrophysics lecture went over my head.
Vineetha loves modern art. She knows it inside out.
My client wanted a completely new design. I had to put on my thinking cap.
I just can't make heads or tails of this map.
Brad thought he would pass the test easily, but he only scraped by.
When Souad took her piano test, she passed with flying colors.
Astronauts made great strides in space exploration in the 1960s.
Keep rehearsing your speech every day—practice makes perfect.

words.russian: быть выше чьего-то понимания, слишком сложно
words.example_en: The professor's astrophysics lecture went over my head.

words.english: get on like a house on fire
words.russian: спеться с первого дня, отлично поладить
words.example_en: Ramón and Tara get on like a house on fire. They're always talking and laughing.
I struck up a friendship with Pete while we were in cooking class last year.
My wife and I hit it off immediately when we first met at a country music concert.
Donna was a fair-weather friend. She wouldn't help when my house flooded.

words.english: get off on the wrong foot
words.example_en: Cy and I got off on the wrong foot on our date. He was an hour late and forgot my name.
Bob reminds me so much of his father. He is a chip off the old block.
Uncle Tony is the black sheep of the family. He's been to prison three times.
We're a close-knit family. We always meet up for birthdays and holidays.

words.russian: это у нас семейное, передаётся по наследству
words.example_en: Curly hair runs in our family, and my daughter, mom, and I all have it.
Francisco thinks the world of his grandson. He loves spending time with him.
I don't think you're really too sick to go to school. You always cry wolf.

words.russian: слон в комнате — очевидная проблема, которую все делают вид, что не замечают
words.example_en: Our falling profits are the elephant in the room at every team meeting.

words.russian: умаслил её, льстил ей ради услуги
words.example_en: I wanted Jo to water my plants when I was on vacation, so I buttered her up by saying her backyard looked nice.

words.russian: ходить на цыпочках — вести себя очень осторожно, чтобы не обидеть
words.example_en: You have to walk on eggshells around Dylan. He gets upset so easily.
I'm glad my boss and I see eye to eye about recycling in the office.

words.russian: не в ладах, расходятся во мнениях
words.example_en: Nelson and Carole are at odds about which car to buy.
Yoshiko offered a sympathetic ear when my cat went missing.

words.russian: бросить в беде, подвести в трудную минуту
words.example_en: Pedro quit his job at the store, leaving me in the lurch.
My friends ribbed me for having such an old phone.

words.russian: готовы вцепиться друг другу в глотку
words.example_en: They can't stop arguing. They're always at each other's throats.

words.russian: иметь зуб на кого-то, личные счёты
words.example_en: I have an axe to grind with my roommate. She's always playing loud music at night.
Our daughter dropped a bombshell when she told us she and her new boyfriend were engaged.
I always end up crossing swords with my grandfather when we talk about what's happening in the news.

words.russian: поворачивать нож в ране, усугублять и без того плохую ситуацию
words.example_en: My boss didn't give me a promotion, and then twisted the knife when she said my report was full of mistakes.
They were so angry with each other that they almost came to blows!

words.russian: не убивать гонца, принёсшего дурную весть
words.example_en: I have some very bad news, but please don't shoot the messenger!

words.russian: украсть чужую славу, перетянуть внимание на себя
words.example_en: Megan stole my thunder when she told the professor the result of my experiment.
I wish my boss would stop beating around the bush and tell me what he really thinks.
We were just shooting the breeze on the porch.
You're very quiet today - cat got your tongue?

words.russian: мы друг друга не так поняли, вышла путаница
words.example_en: I think we got our wires crossed about the meeting time.
Pedro's going to marry Elena. I heard it from the horse's mouth.
A little bird told me that Richa is going to be promoted.

words.english: let the cat out of the bag
words.example_en: Don't let the cat out of the bag!

words.english: take something with a grain of salt
words.russian: относиться с долей скепсиса, не принимать на веру
words.example_en: When news stories are shocking, I always take them with a grain of salt.

words.russian: слухами земля полнится, узнать по слухам
words.example_en: I heard it through the grapevine that Luisa is pregnant.
After leaving the band, Marco dished the dirt about the other band members to a journalist.
Why did you spill the beans and tell Luke I had a new boyfriend?

words.russian: держать язык за зубами, не разглашать
words.example_en: Tania told me a secret but asked me to keep it under my hat.

words.russian: держать в тайне, под покровом тайны
words.example_en: We're keeping our new car model under wraps until the launch later this week.

words.russian: сделать что-то за спиной у кого-то
words.example_en: My wife was angry when I went behind her back and bought myself a new motorcycle.
The salesman really took us for a ride when he sold us this car. It's always breaking down!

words.russian: пропустить мимо ушей, остаться без внимания
words.example_en: I told my daughter she should work harder, but my advice fell on deaf ears.
Omar lent a sympathetic ear when I broke up with my boyfriend.
Ethan was only listening with half an ear and kept checking his phone as I spoke to him.
When my parents got wind of the fact that I'd had a house party, they were furious.
The police chief put us in the picture about the bank robbery.

words.english: be left out of the picture
words.example_en: Arjun felt left out of the picture when his roommates got a dog without telling him.

words.russian: обращаться с кем-то очень бережно и деликатно
words.example_en: Pedro's upset about failing the test. You'll have to handle him with kid gloves.
It was the first time Raul had been caught speeding, so the police let him off the hook.
It's the children who rule the roost in Kaylee's family. They don't respect her.
Chrissie calls the shots in our restaurant. She decides what's on the menu.
Leah's manager twisted her arm, and she agreed to work late.

words.russian: стоять над душой, дышать в затылок
words.example_en: Jack's boss is always breathing down his neck. He wants more independence.
Michael and Heather had an argument about the rules of the game.

words.english: put your cards on the table
words.example_en: Karen decided to put her cards on the table and tell Ricky that she loved him.
Seth isn't always the most hardworking employee. To put it bluntly, he's lazy.

words.russian: сесть в лужу, ляпнуть не то
words.example_en: I really put my foot in it when I asked if Hazel was Tamsin's mother. They are actually sisters.
We were stranded on the rocks until the lifeguards came to the rescue.

words.english: a picture is worth a thousand words
words.russian: лучше один раз увидеть, чем сто раз услышать
words.example_en: Just send her the photo—a picture is worth a thousand words.

words.english: absence makes the heart grow fonder
words.russian: разлука усиливает чувства к любимому человеку
words.example_en: They spent a year apart, and absence made the heart grow fonder.

words.english: two heads are better than one
words.russian: одна голова хорошо, а две лучше
words.example_en: Let's plan this together; two heads are better than one.

words.english: you can lead a horse to water, but you can't make it drink
words.russian: можно создать условия, но нельзя заставить человека ими воспользоваться
words.example_en: I gave him good advice, but you can lead a horse to water, not make it drink.

words.english: you can't judge a book by its cover
words.russian: не суди о книге по обложке
words.example_en: He seemed strict at first, but you can't judge a book by its cover.

words.english: take a page out of someone's book
words.example_en: I'm going to take a page out of your book and clean my desk.

words.russian: разжёвывать и класть в рот, делать всё за кого-то
words.example_en: Evan is always asking me what to do — I feel like I have to spoon-feed him.
We ask all our interview candidates the same questions to create a level playing field.
George arrived at the restaurant immaculately groomed for his date with Cassie.

words.english: never have a hair out of place
words.example_en: Nalini never has a hair out of place.

words.english: the best thing since sliced bread
words.russian: лучше не придумаешь, самое клёвое, что есть
words.example_en: My kids think this TV show is the best thing since sliced bread.

words.russian: то, что надо, лучше не бывает
words.example_en: The ice cream at Giuseppe's parlor really is the bee's knees!
Walking to work gives me the best of both worlds. It helps me save money and get fit.
The crème de la crème of the fashion world were at the launch party.
Noah's old car is past its prime. He's been driving it for years.

words.english: not all it's cracked up to be
words.russian: не так хорош, как о нём говорят
words.example_en: I know everyone else loves it, but this book is not all that it's cracked up to be.
The museum in my town is nothing to write home about. There are very few exhibits.
My old desktop computer is past its sell-by date. It takes a long time to start up.

words.english: not enough room to swing a cat
words.russian: яблоку негде упасть — очень тесное, маленькое пространство
words.example_en: Clarita's new apartment is tiny. There's not enough room to swing a cat.

words.russian: загорелся мечтой о чём-то, всей душой хочет
words.example_en: Harjit has his heart set on getting a pet.
I haven't booked a restaurant for lunch, so let's play it by ear and find somewhere that looks nice.

words.russian: буквально всё подряд, всё, что только можно
words.example_en: We always take everything but the kitchen sink when we travel.

words.russian: тысяча и одна причина, великое множество
words.example_en: I'll give you a thousand-and-one reasons why you shouldn't buy that house.
The guests began to arrive at the party in dribs and drabs.
Sam bought his brother's business, lock, stock, and barrel.

words.russian: раз, два и обчёлся; большая редкость
words.example_en: Customers were few and far between because of the rain.

words.russian: у чёрта на куличках, отдалённый уголок
words.example_en: The castle is in a far-flung corner of the island. It takes hours to drive there.
The car avoided hitting my bike by just a hair's breadth.

words.russian: не по проторенной дорожке, в глуши
words.example_en: I love to travel off the beaten path when I'm on vacation.
Jennifer won the marathon by a whisker.
I think you'll need to replace your washing machine, but I'll take a look on the off-chance that I can fix it.
The weather forecaster announced that we're in for a chance of rain tomorrow.
It looks like a medal is in the cards for Noriko after that amazing throw.
Kerry will only clean up her room when pigs fly!
We had to leave the beach because it started bucketing down.
There are lots of cafés and restaurants in my neck of the woods.
I'm so tired tonight! I'm going to hit the hay.
The border guard gave my documents the once-over, then let me drive on.
I always cast an eye over my desk to make sure I haven't forgotten anything when I leave the office.

words.english: eat someone out of house and home
words.russian: объесть до нитки, съесть всю еду в доме
words.example_en: Yuka's complaining that her sons have eaten her out of house and home.

words.russian: уплести за обе щеки, съесть быстро и жадно
words.example_en: Paolo was so hungry that he wolfed down that huge burger really quickly.
I washed down my pizza with a glass of lemonade.
Blue cheese is an acquired taste. I still think it's disgusting, but Joe loves it.
We sat in front of the TV stuffing our faces with popcorn.
On the last day of the sales, Sam and Hiromi shopped till they dropped.
The guests made a beeline for all the cakes on the table.
Marion is going to up sticks and move to Bristol.
The ship set sail for China this morning. It will arrive in 20 days.
There was no rain for months, and the land was as dry as a bone.
Roy's new suit fits like a glove. He looks really good.
The party was a black-tie event and everyone was really well dressed.
The day I received my degree was a red-letter day for me.

words.russian: зелёная зона, парки и поля вокруг города
words.example_en: We're protesting against plans to build houses on the green belt in our local area.
It's my birthday, so I'm going to paint the town red with my friends tonight.
Akash usually takes forty winks after he finishes his lunch.
Domenica looked the part for her first day of work at the bank.
My aunt is a pillar of the community. She supports charities and attends local events.
Javier's performance will be a tough act to follow. It was incredible.
I'm afraid this product just isn't up to scratch, Kyle.
His voice is OK, but he's not going to set the world on fire.

words.russian: весь мир у твоих ног — перед тобой множество возможностей
words.example_en: You can do anything now that you've finished college. The world is your oyster.
The negotiating teams stayed up all night hammering out a deal.

words.english: put your heart and soul into something
words.example_en: Salvador loves cooking. He puts his heart and soul into it.

words.russian: взяться за дело с места в карьер
words.example_en: The sales team has hit the ground running since we launched our new smartphone.
The result of the experiment is a foregone conclusion.
I think Ruben's dreams of becoming a pop star are totally pie in the sky.

words.english: like shooting fish in a barrel
words.example_en: Winning that race was like shooting fish in a barrel. Nobody else had trained.

words.russian: с закрытыми глазами, легко и не задумываясь
words.example_en: Alicia's worked in this factory so long she could do her job with her eyes shut.

words.english: be thrown in at the deep end
words.russian: брошенный в омут с головой, без подготовки
words.example_en: Rosa was thrown in at the deep end when she had to teach the worst class in the school on her first day at work.

words.russian: говорить о работе даже вне работы
words.example_en: Even at parties, the engineers always talk shop.
We touched base yesterday to check on the project.

words.russian: попасть в точку, сказать то, что нужно
words.example_en: Isaac's speech at the wedding hit the right note. It was really funny, but no one was offended.
Making sure people know about our concert will help us drum up an audience.

words.english: have something down to an art
words.russian: довёл до совершенства, стал мастером своего дела
words.example_en: Antonio is great at flower arranging. He has it down to an art.

words.russian: на голову впереди, на световые годы обогнал
words.example_en: Dion's new laptop is light-years ahead of his old one.

words.russian: ломать стереотипы, не как у всех
words.example_en: Ping's design for the new library really breaks the mold.
It was so quiet on Monday that we decided to close up shop for the afternoon.
When Juan won the national art prize, it was a feather in his cap.
Our plan to improve sales didn't work, but I have a new plan up my sleeve.
If Gavin doesn't start working harder, we'll have to give him the boot.
After working at the bank for 30 years, it was time for me to hang up my boots.

words.english: burn the candle at both ends
words.example_en: Nicole's burning the candle at both ends. She must be exhausted.
It took a lot of elbow grease to get the old car looking good again.
Adele's parents pulled out all the stops to make sure she had a wonderful 18th birthday.
If you cut corners to save time, the quality of your work will suffer.
Carmen is not pulling her weight. I found her asleep at her desk again!

words.russian: привести дело в движение, дать делу ход
words.example_en: I want to buy a house this year, so I've set the wheels in motion and arranged some viewings.
Our company has blazed a trail with these new environmentally friendly cars.
Melissa did really well in the race. She won it hands down.

words.russian: добиться большого успеха, выбиться в люди
words.example_en: Malik's band has hit the big time. It performed at the national stadium last night.
Tyler has finally reached the dizzy heights of head chef. He started out doing the dishes.
The dancers brought the house down with their fantastic performance.
The outdoor exhibition has been a runaway success. Lots of people have come to see it.
Lucy's beauty salon is going from strength to strength. She's opening another soon.
The soccer tournament ran like clockwork. Everyone really enjoyed it.
I knew Helena would go places as an athlete. She was so good at running at school.
Erica's presentation worked like a charm with the investors. They loved the designs.
I'm up to my eyeballs in paperwork this week.

words.english: get a foot in the door
words.example_en: This internship helped him get a foot in the door.

words.english: have your ducks in a row
words.russian: разложить всё по полочкам, быть готовым
words.example_en: Make sure you have your ducks in a row before the meeting.
It's hard to hold down a job with such an unstable schedule.
New rules created a level playing field for small businesses.
He's a big cheese in the marketing industry now.
He quit his job to escape the rat race.
After the merger, she became the top dog.
Near the end of the race, Axel passed the other athletes and took the lead.
Sakura has been putting the heat on the contractors to finish the job quickly.
When our CEO retired, Nisha paid tribute to her with a wonderful speech.

words.english: a chain is only as strong as its weakest link
words.example_en: Remember, a chain is only as strong as its weakest link.

words.english: the early bird catches the worm
words.russian: кто рано встаёт, тому бог подаёт
words.example_en: She arrived first and got the job—the early bird catches the worm.

words.english: a bad workman always blames his tools
words.example_en: He blamed his laptop for the bad essay—a bad workman always blames his tools.
We finished the garden fast—many hands make light work.

words.english: get something down to an art
words.example_en: Antonio is great at flower arranging — he has it down to an art.
Dion's new laptop is light-years ahead of his old one.
Sakura has been putting the heat on the contractors to finish the job quickly.
Losing my office job was a blessing in disguise. My new job is much more interesting.
I was stuck in traffic for three hours on a boiling hot day. It was a fate worse than death!
It's still rough around the edges, but this is my design for the park.

words.russian: обращаться не по адресу, ошибаться в направлении усилий
words.example_en: You're barking up the wrong tree if you think I can help you with your homework.

words.english: make a pig's ear of something
words.example_en: I tried to fix the photocopier, but I've really made a pig's ear of it.

words.english: have your cake and eat it too
words.russian: нельзя и рыбку съесть, и косточкой не подавиться — нельзя получить выгоду без издержек
words.example_en: I bought so many clothes that I can't afford a vacation. I suppose you can't have your cake and eat it too.

words.english: bite off more than you can chew
words.russian: взвалил на себя больше, чем смог осилить
words.example_en: I'm really stressed! I think I bit off more than I could chew when I took this promotion.
Who takes the dog for a walk is a bone of contention for our kids.
I tried to smooth things over between Pari and Toni after their argument.
I decided to hold fire before booking such an expensive holiday.

words.russian: стиснуть зубы и сделать неприятное дело
words.example_en: Balwant had to bite the bullet and tell his teacher that he hadn't done his homework.
Trying to make my children eat more vegetables has been an uphill battle.
Poor you! You've really been in the wars!

words.russian: под огнём критики, на линии огня
words.example_en: The boss is in a terrible mood today! We're all going to be in the line of fire.
Audrey looks really stressed. I think I dodged a bullet when she got that promotion and I didn't.
I lost my phone when I was shopping. It's a long shot, but I'm going to ask if anyone has handed it in at the store.
My home improvements opened a can of worms. The builder discovered so many problems.

words.english: make a mountain out of a molehill
words.example_en: Don't make a mountain out of a molehill. I only asked you to help me bake a cake!
There have been teething problems with the new computer system at work.

words.russian: не по силам, не в своей стихии
words.example_en: Bashir was totally out of his depth on his first day working at the florist.

words.english: like getting blood from a stone
words.example_en: I tried to make the politician answer my question, but it was like getting blood from a stone.
The route through the jungle was fraught with danger.

words.russian: ходить по тонкому льду, сильно рисковать
words.example_en: You're skating on thin ice, Dai. You've arrived late for school every day this week.
Blake is teetering on the brink of losing his job. His boss has given him a final warning.

words.russian: путь свободен, можно действовать без риска
words.example_en: The bear had left, and the coast was clear. We could continue with our trek.

words.english: take your life in your hands
words.russian: рисковать головой, ставить жизнь на кон
words.example_en: You have to take your life in your hands every time you cross this road.

words.russian: на волосок от беды, чудом пронесло
words.example_en: Hayley had a close shave. That ball almost hit her!
Marco had a narrow escape, but managed to avoid being hit by the truck.
Jill's operation went well, but she's not out of the woods yet.
Logan's restaurant is in dire straits. There are never any customers.

words.english: come up against a brick wall
words.russian: упереться в стену, зайти в тупик
words.example_en: The police officer came up against a brick wall when he asked if there were any witnesses to the crime.
Not being able to read music was a stumbling block when I started learning the saxophone.
Tina was in a no-win situation. Her son wanted a beach vacation, but her daughter wanted to go skiing.

words.english: between a rock and a hard place
words.example_en: I have been offered a more interesting job, but the pay is terrible. I'm caught between a rock and a hard place.
The protesters want to stop the building being demolished, but they're fighting a losing battle.
This politician used to be really popular, but now the tide has turned.

words.english: can't see the forest for the trees
words.example_en: Kieran is so involved in every detail of this project that he can't see the forest for the trees.
The boss stirred up a hornet's nest when she said we would have to take a pay cut.
Dennis broke his mom's laptop. He had to face the music and tell her.

words.russian: сделать всё возможное, перевернуть всё вверх дном
words.example_en: The police left no stone unturned in their efforts to solve the crime.
The hikers forged ahead in spite of the terrible snowstorm.

words.english: run around like a headless chicken
words.example_en: I'm running around like a headless chicken trying to get the meal ready for my guests tonight.

words.russian: начать всё с чистого листа заново
words.example_en: The client hates my design for the company logo, so it's back to the drawing board.
Despite being rude to a customer, Ed only received a slap on the wrist, and got a verbal warning.
It was the day of reckoning. The judge was about to sentence Mason for burglary.
The vandals got their just deserts and had to do community service.

words.russian: толочь воду в ступе, заниматься бесполезным делом
words.example_en: Tim's beating a dead horse. Nobody wants to buy his old car.
Margaret's performance fell flat on its face. She can't sing!
My dinner party started to go sideways when I spilled soup everywhere.
Nicola's decision to take her dog shopping turned out to be a recipe for disaster.

words.russian: на грани провала, в тяжёлом положении
words.example_en: Kevin's career is on the ropes. His boss has given him an official warning.
The gang's plan to rob the bank went belly up when a policeman recognized Mike.
The project was delayed by endless red tape.
I set a trap to catch the mouse in the kitchen.
Silvio called the fire department after he accidentally set fire to his shed.

words.russian: любопытной варваре на базаре нос оторвали
words.example_en: Don't ask so many questions—curiosity killed the cat, remember?

words.english: a problem shared is a problem halved
words.example_en: Tell me what's wrong—a problem shared is a problem halved.
There's a gray area in the office dress code. My boss said it was casual, but she told me I couldn't wear shorts.

words.english: you win some, you lose some
words.example_en: I've failed my driving test for the tenth time. You win some, you lose some!
I think it's time to throw in the towel.

words.english: look like butter wouldn't melt in your mouth
words.example_en: Edie looks like butter wouldn't melt in her mouth, but she's actually really naughty.
My aunt believed in keeping up appearances despite having little money.
Chetan is as bold as brass. He walked up to the actor and asked for an autograph.
It was great to see Jasmine at the party. She's always a barrel of laughs.

words.english: too smart for your own good
words.russian: умничает не в меру, себе же во вред
words.example_en: Zoe loves proving how much she knows. She's too smart for her own good.

words.english: wouldn't say boo to a goose
words.russian: робкий как мышь, слова лишнего не скажет от застенчивости
words.example_en: Tony wouldn't say boo to a goose. He gets really shy when he talks to customers.

words.english: your bark is worse than your bite
words.russian: не так страшен чёрт, как его малюют
words.example_en: Don't be afraid of Linda. Her bark is worse than her bite.
Ben never told us he could tap dance. He is such a dark horse.
Hassan is the salt of the earth. I saw him help an old lady cross the road.

words.russian: смотрит на всех свысока, занимает позу морального превосходства
words.example_en: Rachel always gets on her high horse about being a vegetarian. It's so annoying!

words.russian: человек одного таланта, умеет только одно
words.example_en: Ruby is a one-trick pony. She's a great singer, but she can't dance or act.

words.english: know what side your bread is buttered on
words.russian: знает свою выгоду, умеет угодить нужному человеку
words.example_en: Damien makes coffee for his boss every morning. He knows what side his bread is buttered on!

words.russian: публично признать свою неправоту или провал
words.example_en: The head of sales said he would double our profits, so he had to eat humble pie when he told us they had fallen.
Semir is generous to a fault. He invited us into his home for a wonderful meal.

words.russian: вести себя примерно, на самом лучшем поведении
words.example_en: I was on my best behavior when I met my boyfriend's parents.

words.russian: повести себя не в своём духе, странно для себя
words.example_en: Jean behaved out of character at the office party. She's normally so quiet!
I was really upset when a customer shouted at me, but I managed to keep a stiff upper lip.
Our new intern is such an eager beaver. She works really hard.

words.english: have eyes in the back of your head
words.example_en: When you're a parent, you need to have eyes in the back of your head!

words.english: bury your head in the sand
words.russian: прятать голову в песок, как страус
words.example_en: Our profits keep falling, but our boss is burying his head in the sand and not doing anything about it.
Sonia stuck to her guns and told her daughter that she couldn't go to the party.
Jerry is a loose cannon. We should never have invited him to dinner with the clients.
I've already bought you ice cream. Don't push your luck!
I hate going to the dentist, but I know I have to grin and bear it.

words.english: make a rod for your own back
words.example_en: If you let your children eat whatever they want for dinner, you're making a rod for your own back.

words.russian: красиво говорить, но не всегда делать
words.example_en: He talks the talk, but does he mean it?
You're always late too - she can talk!

words.english: talk your way out of something
words.russian: выкрутиться из ситуации враньём или шуткой
words.example_en: He always talks his way out of trouble somehow.
My grandfather loves to spin a yarn about his travels.
Salespeople usually have the gift of the gab.
The voting process in this election is completely above board. All the votes are counted and checked.
I think Warren was bending the truth when I interviewed him for this job.
David lied through his teeth to his mom. He claimed the cat had broken her favorite vase.

words.russian: следить не отрывая глаз, глаз не спускать
words.example_en: The examiner watched us like a hawk, making sure we didn't cheat.
The teacher turned a blind eye to his students' bad behavior.
You've changed your tune! I thought you didn't like cats.
Ramona loves to toot her own horn. She's always showing us her trophies.

words.english: take a page out of your book
words.russian: брать пример с кого-то, перенимать хорошую привычку
words.example_en: I'm going to take a page out of your book and clean my desk.
Ian's become too big for his boots since his promotion. He always ignores me.
Carter is such a clever clogs. He's always the first to raise his hand in class.
Julia used to be so disorganized, but has really got her act together recently.

words.english: keep on the straight and narrow
words.example_en: Since leaving jail, Crystal has kept on the straight and narrow.

words.english: take the law into your own hands
words.example_en: Seth took the law into his own hands and confronted the vandals.
Carla refused to toe the line and wear the same uniform as the other students.
We decided to take a chance with the weather and set off on our hike.
Mateo set an example for his children and ate all his vegetables.
Judy was sad to see the birds trapped in their cage, so she set them free.
I had to hold my tongue when Julian showed me his new suit. It was horrible!
The children were as good as gold while they stayed with their grandmother.
Just tell the truth—honesty is always the best policy.
I thought my new boss was really nice, but he showed his true colors when I forgot to put sugar in his coffee.

words.english: leave a bad taste in your mouth
words.example_en: Pete's habit of dropping litter leaves a bad taste in my mouth.
Watching golf on TV is so boring. It's like watching paint dry.

words.russian: как кролик перед удавом — застыть от шока или страха
words.example_en: Flo looked like a deer in headlights when I caught her reading my diary.

words.english: like the cat that got the cream
words.russian: очень доволен собой, сияет от радости
words.example_en: Bill looked like the cat that got the cream when he won the competition.

words.russian: действует мне на нервы, бесит меня
words.example_en: It really gets my goat when someone eats smelly food on the train.

words.russian: как с гуся вода — совершенно не задевает, не расстраивает
words.example_en: Jenny didn't mind when her friends laughed at her coat. It was water off a duck's back.

words.russian: слезами горю не поможешь — расстраиваться из-за того, что уже случилось
words.example_en: Don't get so upset about burning the cookies. It's no use crying over spilled milk.

words.russian: это уже переходит все границы, это худшее из всего
words.example_en: I know Aiden's always late, but arriving late for his own wedding takes the cake!

words.russian: зелен виноград — завистливое и обиженное отношение
words.example_en: Fiona told me she doesn't like my new scooter. I think it's just sour grapes.
Don jumped down my throat when I suggested he clean the kitchen.

words.russian: устроил мне разнос, отчитал по полной
words.example_en: My wife read me the riot act for forgetting to lock the door again.

words.english: wear your heart on your sleeve
words.example_en: You can always tell when he's upset. He wears his heart on his sleeve.
People in my town are up in arms about plans to build a new supermarket.
When Dylan copied her homework, Zoe was so angry that she was foaming at the mouth.
After a long week at work, I let my hair down at my best friend's party.
I get itchy feet when I haven't been abroad for a long time.
Akio was full of beans when he started his new job at the salon.
I had egg on my face after I called Jo's new boyfriend Ben. That was her ex-boyfriend's name!
The sight of the park in spring is a joy to behold.

words.russian: рваться в бой, гореть желанием начать
words.example_en: Two minutes before the race, the athletes were all raring to go.
Robbie has flung himself into training for the gymnastics competition.
Clara was bright-eyed and bushy-tailed as she set off on the hike.
We waited with bated breath for the judge to announce the winner.

words.russian: лезть на стену от избытка энергии
words.example_en: The children were bouncing off the walls after a whole day indoors.

words.english: have a lump in your throat
words.example_en: Yannis had a lump in his throat when he said goodbye to his son.

words.russian: с тяжёлым сердцем, с грустью на душе
words.example_en: It was with a heavy heart that I decided to leave my job at the gallery.
It makes my blood boil when people litter.
I kicked myself when I realized I had left my bag on the train.

words.russian: выйти из себя, взорваться от злости
words.example_en: Dad hit the roof when he heard I'd failed my tests again.

words.russian: рвать и метать, быть в боевом настрое
words.example_en: Our boss is on the warpath about people leaving their computers on overnight.
Jen got that sinking feeling when she realized she had lost her keys.
That horror movie sent shivers down my spine. It was so scary!
My blood ran cold when I heard a strange noise outside.

words.english: make your hair stand on end
words.example_en: The sight of the old, spooky house made my hair stand on end.
Alice was shaking in her boots before she did her first bungee jump.

words.english: want the earth to swallow you up
words.example_en: Nina wanted the earth to swallow her up when her dad showed her boyfriend pictures of her as a baby.

words.english: with your tail between your legs
words.example_en: The players left the field with their tails between their legs after losing the game.
Paolo almost jumped out of his skin when a bat flew into his room.

words.russian: не особо на это рассчитывай, вряд ли это скоро случится
words.example_en: Isabella says she will pass her English test, but don't hold your breath!
When Ava told me how much fun she'd had at the party, it rubbed salt into the wound. I wasn't invited.
She finally told me the truth to get it off her chest.
I have a sneaking suspicion that Marcia was at a job interview this morning.
I knew Frida hadn't been out sick. I smelled a rat when she came back to work with a suntan.
The children are being very quiet. I think there's something fishy going on!

words.russian: если бы взглядом можно было убить
words.example_en: Kezia looked furious when I told her I'd torn her dress. If looks could kill!

words.russian: витать в облаках, мыслями где-то далеко
words.example_en: Martina didn't hear the teacher's question. She was a million miles away.
Austin needed some retail therapy, so he spent the afternoon at the mall.

words.russian: даю голову на отсечение, что этого не случится
words.example_en: If Sven wins the race, I'll eat my hat!

words.english: take your hat off to someone
words.russian: снимаю шляпу перед кем-то, искренне восхищаюсь
words.example_en: I take my hat off to Jo for collecting so much money for charity.
My boss got very hot under the collar when I handed my report in late.

words.english: have a bee in your bonnet
words.russian: зациклиться на чём-то, быть одержимым идеей
words.example_en: Jane has a bee in her bonnet about people dropping litter on the sidewalk.
Mark holds a grudge against me because I didn't give him a promotion last year.
